title: Aurelius, Meditations §aur_07_xli: Can the Gods, who are immortal, for the continuance of so many ages bear with...
body: Can the Gods, who are immortal, for the continuance of so many ages bear without indignation with such and so many sinners, as have ever been, yea not only so, but also take such care for them, that they want nothing; and dust thou so grievously take on, as one that could bear with them no longer; thou that art but for a moment of time? yea thou that art one of those sinners thyself? A very ridiculous thing it is, that any man should dispense with vice and wickedness in himself, which is in his power to restrain; and should go about to suppress it in others, which is altogether impossible.
author: engine

witnesses.label: Vaticanus Graecus 1950 — earliest complete Greek MS
Dalfen, Marci Aurelii Antonini Ad Se Ipsum Libri XII (Teubner 1987)
George Long translation (1862) — public domain English
